channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Microservices Didn't Fail. People Did
item.description: The pattern was sound. The history is clear. What broke microservices wasn't the architecture — it was practitioners who applied the deployment unit without the engineering discipline the original...

item.title: 400+ Remote Companies Using React in 2026
item.description: React is the default. 409 companies on Remoet use it. That is more than four times the next-closest frontend framework on the platform. If you write React, you are not in a special pool. You are...

item.title: Gizmo Guard - Safeguard Bot (Powered by Gemma4)
item.description: This is a submission for the Gemma 4 Challenge: Build with Gemma 4 What I Built GizmoGuard is a low-budget, privacy-first AI-at-the-edge personal safety and monitoring bot powered by locally...

item.title: Gizmo Guard - Safeguard Bot (Powered by Gemma4)
item.description: This is a submission for the Gemma 4 Challenge: Build with Gemma 4 What I Built GizmoGuard is a low-budget, privacy-first AI-at-the-edge personal safety and monitoring bot powered by locally...

item.title: Grafana 'No Data' after migration: 7 reconcilers we had to kill first
item.description: The first fix lasted 90 seconds. We had corrected the Grafana datasource URL from prometheus:9999 back to prometheus:9090, watched the pod roll, refreshed the dashboard, and seen one panel come...

item.title: CrimsonOS: Building a Mobile OS from the Firmware Up
item.description: The Problem NetHunter is solid, but it's still Android underneath. You inherit the vendor blob stack, the Google services layer, and a UI that wasn't designed for launching msfconsole or...

item.title: I’ve Been Building a Python Game Engine
item.description: I’ve been working on a project called ABS Engine, a Python game engine focused on keeping projects simple and organized. Instead of making developers write massive setup code, games are mostly...

item.title: Counting tokens is dumb. So we built a free metric for AI proficiency.
item.description: We’ve been trying to figure out a real answer to a question that keeps coming up: how do you measure whether someone is actually good at Claude Code, Codex, and the other AI coding tools? Not "do...

item.title: Best Free AI Tools for Developers in 2026
item.description: AI coding tools are no longer locked behind expensive subscriptions. In 2026, developers can access genuinely powerful free AI tools for coding, debugging, documentation, architecture planning,...

item.title: I Replaced ChatGPT With Gemma 4 In My Product. It Felt Like The Same Radio Show With A Different Host.
item.description: This is a submission for the Gemma 4 Challenge: Build with Gemma 4 Most “read later” links quietly die in browser tabs. At some point I realized I wasn’t actually trying to consume more content...

item.title: Meta Is in Crisis, Google Search’s Makeover, and AI Gets Booed by Graduates
item.description: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, we unpack the mass layoffs at Meta, big announcements at Google I/O, and the latest backlash against AI.

item.title: Roundtables: Can AI Learn to Understand the World?
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below AI companies want to build systems that understand the external world and overcome the limitations of LLMs. Recent developments have brought world models to...

item.title: All of the updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s battle over OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of...

item.title: Firefox is working on a rounded redesign with easy-to-find controls for privacy and AI
item.description: Firefox is getting a drastic visual overhaul with a redesigned Settings section that will make it easier to find and use privacy settings, including the switch for turning off all present and...

item.title: In desperate times, graduates find hope in humiliating tech CEOs
item.description: University graduates are booing and heckling corporate executives who praise AI during their commencement ceremonies, and the only people who seem to be genuinely surprised by this are the...

item.title: Who gets to own the Luigi Mangione story?
item.description: On Monday morning, a judge overseeing the New York state case on the killing of the UnitedHealthcare CEO ruled that some evidence collected by police could not be shown to a jury. It wasn't the...

item.title: Spotify and Universal Music strike deal allowing fan-made AI covers and remixes
item.description: Spotify is partnering with Universal Music Group￼ to let Premium subscribers create AI-generated song covers and remixes, with participating artists receiving a share of the revenue.

item.title: Philips’ new display has a screen on both sides
item.description: Its name might be dull and uninspired, but the Philips 24B2D5300 Business Monitor brings a novel feature I've never seen on a display before: screens on either side. The design will primarily...

item.title: Six search engines worth trying now that Google isn’t really Google anymore
item.description: Google is about to look really different, and if you're not a fan of the AI overview feature, then you're not going to like what's coming.

item.title: Scaling creativity in the age of AI
item.description: Storytelling is core to humanity’s DNA, stemming from our impulse to express ideals, warnings, hopes, and experiences. Technology has always been woven through the medium and the distribution:...

item.title: One of Meta’s big legal reckonings just ended in a settlement
item.description: After back-to-back losses in trials grappling with its impact on teens' mental health, Meta just settled what was supposed to be its next legal battle with Kentucky's Breathitt County School...

item.title: Bungie gives up on Destiny
item.description: Bungie is moving on from Destiny 2. The studio announced Thursday that it's releasing the last major update for the game on June 9th as its focus "turns towards a new beginning for Bungie." After...

item.title: Trump delays AI security executive order: ‘I don’t want to get in the way of that leading’
item.description: President Trump delayed signing an executive order that would have required pre-release government security reviews of AI models, citing dissatisfaction with the order's language.

item.title: Sonos’s pint-sized Roam 2 speaker is 25 percent off for Memorial Day
item.description: If your summer plans involve spending any time outdoors, whether on the trail or at a pool party, a good portable Bluetooth speaker can go a long way. The latest Sonos Roam is built precisely for...

item.title: This AI guitar pedal let me roll my own effects
item.description: I'm not sure anyone was really asking for an AI guitar pedal. But it was inevitable that someone would build one. One of the first to take the plunge is Polyend, a well-respected music gear maker...

item.title: Uber is deploying its own self-driving cars again, just not as robotaxis
item.description: Uber is putting its own autonomous vehicles back on the road as part of its new AV Lab project to collect data for its dozens of robotaxi partners. The cars will be fitted with all the sensors...

item.title: macOS Kernel Memory Corruption Exploit
item.description: A group used Anthropic’s Mythos AI model to help find a kernel memory corruption vulnerability and exploit on Apple’s M5. News article.

item.title: I Cloned Myself With Gemini’s AI Avatar Tool. The Result Was Unnervingly Me
item.description: I used the Gemini app to generate lifelike videos featuring a digital clone of myself. Google sees this as the future of creation. I’m still creeped out.

item.title: Spotify adds AI-powered Q&A and briefing generation features to podcasts
item.description: Spotify will let you generate daily or weekly briefs based on your prompts

item.title: Spotify takes on Google’s NotebookLM with its new app
item.description: Spotify is releasing the new desktop app as a research preview in more than 20 markets.
